rows.Heading: Preflop Opening Ranges: Overview
rows.BodyText: Open tighter early, wider late. ~2-2.5bb online, 3bb+ live. GTO baseline: UTG ~16-18%, HJ ~21%, CO ~25-28%, BTN ~40-44%, SB raise-or-fold. Opens are SMALLER from EP in modern solver play because more players remain to 3-bet.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Key numbers to know
rows.BodyText: UTG opens ~13.4% (RFI) at 100bb 6-max (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. HJ opens ~18.2% (RFI) at 100bb 6-max (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. CO opens ~23.3% (RFI) at 100bb 6-max (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. BTN opens ~38.5% (RFI) at 100bb 6-max (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. SB opens ~40.2% (RFI) at 100bb 6-max (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. UTG opens ~13.4% (RFI) at 100bb 6-max (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: How to apply it
rows.BodyText: Open tighter early, wider late. Baseline: UTG ~16-18%, HJ ~21%, CO ~25-28%, BTN ~40-44%, SB raise-or-fold. Opens are SMALLER from EP in modern solver play because more players remain to 3-bet GTO baseline: UTG ~16-18%, HJ ~21%, CO ~25-28%, BTN ~40-44%, SB raise-or-fold. Opens are SMALLER from EP in modern solver play because more players remain to 3-bet. Exploit: tighten and let them pay you off postflop; isolate their limps Population: low-stakes opens are too loose & too big from EP. Exploit: tighten and let them pay you off postflop; isolate their limps. Coach by drilling position-by-position to 90%.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Common mistakes & fixes
rows.BodyText: Opening too wide from EP; using one big size from every seat; limping outside the SB. Players lean on static memorized charts and ignore position, stack depth, and how many players are left to act. See leak LEAK-001 for the remediation path.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Practice & assess
rows.BodyText: Drill this with quiz Q00001. Mastery is assessed against objective LO-001. Review ranges at RNG-0001.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Blind Defense Basics: Overview
rows.BodyText: BB defends widest because it closes action. GTO baseline: vs 2.5bb BTN ~44% defend; vs 3bb ~33%; BvB 50%+. Flat = suited/connected, 3-bet = polar. OOP realization is poor so defense is below MDF.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Key numbers to know
rows.BodyText: BB defends widest because it closes action. (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: How to apply it
rows.BodyText: BB defends widest because it closes action. Baseline: vs 2.5bb BTN ~44% defend; vs 3bb ~33%; BvB 50%+. Flat = suited/connected, 3-bet = polar. OOP realization is poor so defense is below MDF GTO baseline: vs 2.5bb BTN ~44% defend; vs 3bb ~33%; BvB 50%+. Flat = suited/connected, 3-bet = polar. OOP realization is poor so defense is below MDF. Exploit: c-bet/barrel more, value thinner; 3-bet them lighter Population: micro BBs over-fold flop to c-bets and 3-bet too little. Exploit: c-bet/barrel more, value thinner; 3-bet them lighter. Coach pot-odds + realization.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Common mistakes & fixes
rows.BodyText: Defending too wide with unplayable offsuit hands; never 3-betting; over-folding vs small opens. Players lean on static memorized charts and ignore position, stack depth, and how many players are left to act. See leak LEAK-001 for the remediation path.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Practice & assess
rows.BodyText: Drill this with quiz Q00357. Mastery is assessed against objective LO-002. Review ranges at BBD-0001.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: C-Bet Fundamentals: Overview
rows.BodyText: Who's favored decides sizing. Small on range boards, polar on static. GTO baseline: with range+nut advantage bet small & often; without it, check more. Nut advantage (more nutted combos) enables polarization/overbets.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Key numbers to know
rows.BodyText: Who's favored decides sizing. (Calibrated; source: Synthesized). Not solver-exact. 3-Bet Pot In Position: flop c-bet ~71.0% (avg over boards) (Calibrated; source: Upswing / Run It Once / Janda). Not solver-exact. 3-Bet Pot Out of Position: flop c-bet ~71.0% (avg over boards) (Calibrated; source: Upswing / Run It Once / Janda). Not solver-exact. Single Raised Pot In Position: flop c-bet ~68.7% (avg over boards) (Calibrated; source: Upswing / Run It Once / Janda). Not solver-exact. Single Raised Pot Out of Position: flop c-bet ~68.7% (avg over boards) (Calibrated; source: Upswing / Run It Once / Janda). Not solver-exact.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: How to apply it
rows.BodyText: Who's favored decides sizing. Baseline: with range+nut advantage bet small & often; without it, check more. Nut advantage (more nutted combos) enables polarization/overbets GTO baseline: with range+nut advantage bet small & often; without it, check more. Nut advantage (more nutted combos) enables polarization/overbets. Exploit: c-bet/barrel more, value thinner, bluff rivers less Population: pools over-fold flop yet over-call rivers. Exploit: c-bet/barrel more, value thinner, bluff rivers less. Coach board-favored reads.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Common mistakes & fixes
rows.BodyText: Auto-c-betting every board; using one size; betting big without nut advantage. Players judge hands by absolute strength and auto-pilot, instead of reading board texture and whose range the board favors. See leak LEAK-012 for the remediation path.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Practice & assess
rows.BodyText: Drill this with quiz QA0406. Mastery is assessed against objective LO-007. Review ranges at FCB-0001.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Bankroll & Mindset: Overview
rows.BodyText: Keep decisions EV-based, not emotional. Roll sized to game & variance. Baseline: identify tilt triggers, set a stop-loss, quit-on-tilt rule, and a warm-up routine to protect A-game.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Key numbers to know
rows.BodyText: Keep decisions EV-based, not emotional. (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. Roll sized to game & variance. (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: How to apply it
rows.BodyText: Keep decisions EV-based, not emotional. Baseline: Baseline: identify tilt triggers, set a stop-loss, quit-on-tilt rule, and a warm-up routine to protect A-game. Baseline: identify tilt triggers, set a stop-loss, quit-on-tilt rule, and a warm-up routine to protect A-game. Exploit: Deviate from the baseline toward the pool's specific tendency. Coach a tilt profile + stop-loss; exploit tilted opponents (value heavy, don't bluff).
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Common mistakes & fixes
rows.BodyText: Revenge-spewing a specific player; chasing losses; playing through tilt. Emotional and cognitive biases (loss-chasing, results-orientation, ego) override disciplined, EV-based decisions. See leak LEAK-083 for the remediation path.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Practice & assess
rows.BodyText: Drill this with quiz Q01446. Mastery is assessed against objective LO-017. Review ranges at -.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Tournament Basics: Overview
rows.BodyText: Switch strategy by depth. More dead money → widen. GTO baseline: 30-40bb mix flat vs 3-bet; 20-25bb a raise/3-bet-jam-or-fold game; low SPR postflop. Antes widen everything.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Key numbers to know
rows.BodyText: Switch strategy by depth. (Calibrated; source: Synthesized). Not solver-exact. More dead money → widen. (Calibrated; source: Synthesized). Not solver-exact.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: How to apply it
rows.BodyText: Switch strategy by depth. Baseline: 30-40bb mix flat vs 3-bet; 20-25bb a raise/3-bet-jam-or-fold game; low SPR postflop. Antes widen everything GTO baseline: 30-40bb mix flat vs 3-bet; 20-25bb a raise/3-bet-jam-or-fold game; low SPR postflop. Antes widen everything. Exploit: Deviate from the baseline toward the pool's specific tendency. Population: pools over-flat OOP and under-3-bet-jam → resteal wider. Coach the identity of each band.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Common mistakes & fixes
rows.BodyText: Playing 20bb like 100bb; flatting 3-bets at low SPR; deep-postflop habits at 25bb. Players keep deep-stack ranges as stacks shrink and under-use the reshove, because antes and low SPR feel unfamiliar. See leak LEAK-063 for the remediation path.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Practice & assess
rows.BodyText: Drill this with quiz Q01431. Review ranges at MTT-0001.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Push/Fold Introduction: Overview
rows.BodyText: Be the shover, not the caller. GTO baseline: 5bb pure jam-or-fold; 10bb jam UTG ~12-13% → BTN ~38% → SB ~67-75%. Jamming ranges are wider than call-off ranges.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Key numbers to know
rows.BodyText: BTN jams ~46.0% at 10bb (Nash push/fold) (Nash-Solved; source: PokerCoaching). Model-dependent (ICM/payouts). CO jams ~33.0% at 10bb (Nash push/fold) (Nash-Solved; source: PokerCoaching). Model-dependent (ICM/payouts). SB jams ~47.8% at 10bb (Nash push/fold) (Nash-Solved; source: PokerCoaching). Model-dependent (ICM/payouts). UTG jams ~20.1% at 10bb (Nash push/fold) (Nash-Solved; source: PokerCoaching). Model-dependent (ICM/payouts).
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: How to apply it
rows.BodyText: Be the shover, not the caller. Baseline: 5bb pure jam-or-fold; 10bb jam UTG ~12-13% → BTN ~38% → SB ~67-75%. Jamming ranges are wider than call-off ranges GTO baseline: 5bb pure jam-or-fold; 10bb jam UTG ~12-13% → BTN ~38% → SB ~67-75%. Jamming ranges are wider than call-off ranges. Exploit: Deviate from the baseline toward the pool's specific tendency. Population: opponents call jams too wide and reshove too little → jam for fold equity, value-jam vs the callers. Coach charts to 90%.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Common mistakes & fixes
rows.BodyText: Open-folding spots that should jam; min-raise/folding short; over-jamming weak offsuit aces from EP. Players cling to deep-stack habits and fear busting, instead of trusting fold equity and ante math. See leak LEAK-033 for the remediation path.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Practice & assess
rows.BodyText: Drill this with quiz Q00625. Mastery is assessed against objective LO-005. Review ranges at PF-0001.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Ante Adjustments: Overview
rows.BodyText: More dead money → widen. GTO baseline: antes raise the reward of stealing → open/steal/jam wider (~+20-30% hands with full antes).
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Key numbers to know
rows.BodyText: More dead money → widen. (Calibrated; source: Synthesized). Not solver-exact.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: How to apply it
rows.BodyText: More dead money → widen. Baseline: antes raise the reward of stealing → open/steal/jam wider (~+20-30% hands with full antes) GTO baseline: antes raise the reward of stealing → open/steal/jam wider (~+20-30% hands with full antes). Exploit: Deviate from the baseline toward the pool's specific tendency. Population: many players don't widen with antes → out-steal them. Coach ante-adjusted ranges.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Common mistakes & fixes
rows.BodyText: Keeping no-ante ranges once antes appear. Players keep deep-stack ranges as stacks shrink and under-use the reshove, because antes and low SPR feel unfamiliar. See leak LEAK-063 for the remediation path.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Practice & assess
rows.BodyText: Drill this with quiz Q01431. Mastery is assessed against objective LO-014. Review ranges at MTT-0001.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: 3-Bet & 4-Bet Pots: Overview
rows.BodyText: IP ~3-3.5x, OOP ~4x; shape by matchup. Polar: QQ+/AK + blocker bluffs. GTO baseline: 3-bet smaller IP (~3x), larger OOP (~4x). OOP/vs-wide = linear; IP/vs-tight = polar. 3-bet-or-fold from most seats; BTN may flat vs UTG.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Key numbers to know
rows.BodyText: 3-Bet BB vs BTN open: 3-bets ~7.0% of combos (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. 3-Bet BB vs CO open: 3-bets ~5.5% of combos (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. 3-Bet BB vs SB open (BvB): 3-bets ~12.5% of combos (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. 3-Bet BTN vs CO open: 3-bets ~8.3% of combos (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. 3-Bet BTN vs UTG open: 3-bets ~3.7% of combos (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. 3-Bet CO vs HJ open: 3-bets ~7.1% of combos (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: How to apply it
rows.BodyText: IP ~3-3.5x, OOP ~4x; shape by matchup. Baseline: 3-bet smaller IP (~3x), larger OOP (~4x). OOP/vs-wide = linear; IP/vs-tight = polar. 3-bet-or-fold from most seats; BTN may flat vs UTG GTO baseline: 3-bet smaller IP (~3x), larger OOP (~4x). OOP/vs-wide = linear; IP/vs-tight = polar. 3-bet-or-fold from most seats; BTN may flat vs UTG. Exploit: widen value 3-bets + add blocker bluffs Population: pools 3-bet too tight & don't 4-bet bluff, so they over-fold to your 3-bets. Exploit: widen value 3-bets + add blocker bluffs. Coach linear-vs-polar by spot.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Common mistakes & fixes
rows.BodyText: Flatting too much OOP; one-size 3-betting; no bluff 3-bets (face-up value). Players lean on static memorized charts and ignore position, stack depth, and how many players are left to act. See leak LEAK-001 for the remediation path.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Practice & assess
rows.BodyText: Drill this with quiz Q00001. Mastery is assessed against objective LO-018. Review ranges at P3BPIP-0001.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Facing 3-Bets: Overview
rows.BodyText: Fold most; call a continuing range; 4-bet polar. GTO baseline (UTG vs BTN): call ~33.6%, 4-bet ~12.4%, fold >50%. IP defends wider; OOP is more 4-bet-or-fold. AK/QQ often flat vs opens.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Key numbers to know
rows.BodyText: 3-Bet BB vs BTN open: 3-bets ~7.0% of combos (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. 3-Bet BB vs CO open: 3-bets ~5.5% of combos (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. 3-Bet BB vs SB open (BvB): 3-bets ~12.5% of combos (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. 3-Bet BTN vs CO open: 3-bets ~8.3% of combos (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. 3-Bet BTN vs UTG open: 3-bets ~3.7% of combos (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. 3-Bet CO vs HJ open: 3-bets ~7.1% of combos (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: How to apply it
rows.BodyText: Fold most; call a continuing range; 4-bet polar. Baseline: GTO baseline (UTG vs BTN): call ~33. GTO baseline (UTG vs BTN): call ~33.6%, 4-bet ~12.4%, fold >50%. IP defends wider; OOP is more 4-bet-or-fold. AK/QQ often flat vs opens. Exploit: 4-bet-bluff more vs nitty 3-bettors; flat more vs maniacs Population: pools under-4-bet-bluff, so fold your 4-bets to their 5-bet jams unless KK+. Exploit: 4-bet-bluff more vs nitty 3-bettors; flat more vs maniacs. Coach SPR & commitment.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Common mistakes & fixes
rows.BodyText: Calling 3-bets too wide OOP; never 4-bet bluffing; stacking off 100bb with one pair. Players lean on static memorized charts and ignore position, stack depth, and how many players are left to act. See leak LEAK-001 for the remediation path.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Practice & assess
rows.BodyText: Drill this with quiz Q00001. Mastery is assessed against objective LO-019. Review ranges at F3B-0001.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Turn Play: Overview
rows.BodyText: Barrel cards that favor you + carry blockers. Pair value with semi-bluffs OOP. GTO baseline: barrel turns/rivers that improve your range or scare the caller; choose bluffs with equity/blockers that can keep firing.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Key numbers to know
rows.BodyText: Turn barrel ~53.0% after betting flop and getting called (Calibrated; source: Upswing / Run It Once). Not solver-exact. Pair value with semi-bluffs OOP. (Calibrated; source: Synthesized). Not solver-exact.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: How to apply it
rows.BodyText: Barrel cards that favor you + carry blockers. Baseline: barrel turns/rivers that improve your range or scare the caller; choose bluffs with equity/blockers that can keep firing GTO baseline: barrel turns/rivers that improve your range or scare the caller; choose bluffs with equity/blockers that can keep firing. Exploit: Deviate from the baseline toward the pool's specific tendency. Population: over-folders fold turns → barrel more; stations don't → value only. Coach card-by-card re-evaluation.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Common mistakes & fixes
rows.BodyText: Auto-barreling every card; giving up the instant called; bluffing without blockers. Players judge hands by absolute strength and auto-pilot, instead of reading board texture and whose range the board favors. See leak LEAK-012 for the remediation path.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Practice & assess
rows.BodyText: Drill this with quiz QA0406. Mastery is assessed against objective LO-008. Review ranges at TRN-0001.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Population Reads: Overview
rows.BodyText: Don't fold — value relentlessly. Steal & bluff relentlessly. Tendency: call far too wide, rarely fold, under-raise. Exploit: value-bet thin & big, cut bluffs to near zero.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Key numbers to know
rows.BodyText: Don't fold — value relentlessly. (Calibrated; source: Synthesized). Not solver-exact. Steal & bluff relentlessly. (Calibrated; source: Synthesized). Not solver-exact.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: How to apply it
rows.BodyText: Don't fold — value relentlessly. Baseline: Tendency: call far too wide, rarely fold, under-raise. Tendency: call far too wide, rarely fold, under-raise. Exploit: value-bet thin & big, cut bluffs to near zero. Exploit: Deviate from the baseline toward the pool's specific tendency. Coach 'who calls worse?' before betting. Highest-EV adjustment at low stakes.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Common mistakes & fixes
rows.BodyText: Bluffing stations; checking back thin value. Players assume opponents play GTO instead of adjusting to how the specific pool actually deviates. See leak LEAK-012 for the remediation path.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Practice & assess
rows.BodyText: Drill this with quiz Q01416. Mastery is assessed against objective LO-011. Review ranges at PSRPIP-0001.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Reshove & Resteal: Overview
rows.BodyText: Re-jam over a steal with fold equity. GTO baseline: vs a 20bb BTN open the BB can reshove KK-22, AK-AJ, ATo-A9o, A4o-A2o + mixed suited; antes widen it. Core 6-15bb skill.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Key numbers to know
rows.BodyText: UTG opens ~13.4% (RFI) at 100bb 6-max (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. HJ opens ~18.2% (RFI) at 100bb 6-max (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. CO opens ~23.3% (RFI) at 100bb 6-max (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. BTN opens ~38.5% (RFI) at 100bb 6-max (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. SB opens ~40.2% (RFI) at 100bb 6-max (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. BTN jams ~46.0% at 10bb (Nash push/fold) (Nash-Solved; source: PokerCoaching). Model-dependent (ICM/payouts).
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: How to apply it
rows.BodyText: Re-jam over a steal with fold equity. Baseline: vs a 20bb BTN open the BB can reshove KK-22, AK-AJ, ATo-A9o, A4o-A2o + mixed suited; antes widen it. Core 6-15bb skill GTO baseline: vs a 20bb BTN open the BB can reshove KK-22, AK-AJ, ATo-A9o, A4o-A2o + mixed suited; antes widen it. Core 6-15bb skill. Exploit: Deviate from the baseline toward the pool's specific tendency. Population: pools under-reshove → resteal wider; they over-fold to reshoves. Coach depth/position reshove ranges.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Common mistakes & fixes
rows.BodyText: Flatting OOP short (no fold equity); reshoving too tight; ignoring antes. Players cling to deep-stack habits and fear busting, instead of trusting fold equity and ante math. See leak LEAK-033 for the remediation path.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Practice & assess
rows.BodyText: Drill this with quiz Q00625. Mastery is assessed against objective LO-006. Review ranges at PF-0001.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Mid-Stack Play (20-40bb): Overview
rows.BodyText: Switch strategy by depth. GTO baseline: 30-40bb mix flat vs 3-bet; 20-25bb a raise/3-bet-jam-or-fold game; low SPR postflop. Antes widen everything.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Key numbers to know
rows.BodyText: Switch strategy by depth. (Calibrated; source: Synthesized). Not solver-exact.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: How to apply it
rows.BodyText: Switch strategy by depth. Baseline: 30-40bb mix flat vs 3-bet; 20-25bb a raise/3-bet-jam-or-fold game; low SPR postflop. Antes widen everything GTO baseline: 30-40bb mix flat vs 3-bet; 20-25bb a raise/3-bet-jam-or-fold game; low SPR postflop. Antes widen everything. Exploit: Deviate from the baseline toward the pool's specific tendency. Population: pools over-flat OOP and under-3-bet-jam → resteal wider. Coach the identity of each band.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Common mistakes & fixes
rows.BodyText: Playing 20bb like 100bb; flatting 3-bets at low SPR; deep-postflop habits at 25bb. Players keep deep-stack ranges as stacks shrink and under-use the reshove, because antes and low SPR feel unfamiliar. See leak LEAK-063 for the remediation path.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Practice & assess
rows.BodyText: Drill this with quiz Q01431. Mastery is assessed against objective LO-013. Review ranges at MTT-0001.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Bubble Basics: Overview
rows.BodyText: Need more equity to stack off near pay jumps. Ratio of $ lost busting to $ gained winning. GTO baseline: required equity rises from ~33% (chipEV) to ~37-38% (normal bubble) up to ~61% (high bubble factor). ICM is present from hand 1 (~1.8% RP in a 200-runner).
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Key numbers to know
rows.BodyText: ICM chip-EV breakeven ~33.0% (Calibrated; source: GTO Wizard). Not solver-exact. ICM normal-bubble required equity ~37.5% (Calibrated; source: GTO Wizard). Not solver-exact. ICM high-bubble-factor required equity ~61.0% (Calibrated; source: GTO Wizard). Not solver-exact. ICM chip-EV breakeven ~33.0% (Calibrated; source: GTO Wizard). Not solver-exact. ICM normal-bubble required equity ~37.5% (Calibrated; source: GTO Wizard). Not solver-exact. ICM high-bubble-factor required equity ~61.0% (Calibrated; source: GTO Wizard). Not solver-exact.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: How to apply it
rows.BodyText: Need more equity to stack off near pay jumps. Baseline: required equity rises from ~33% (chipEV) to ~37-38% (normal bubble) up to ~61% (high bubble factor). ICM is present from hand 1 (~1.8% RP in a 200-runner) GTO baseline: required equity rises from ~33% (chipEV) to ~37-38% (normal bubble) up to ~61% (high bubble factor). ICM is present from hand 1 (~1.8% RP in a 200-runner). Exploit: Deviate from the baseline toward the pool's specific tendency. Population: recreationals ignore ICM and call too wide → don't bluff them off, let them spew; pressure ICM-aware mid-stacks. Coach risk-premium math.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Common mistakes & fixes
rows.BodyText: Calling off chipEV-wide on bubbles; ignoring pay jumps; flatting when you should fold. Players think in chips rather than real-money equity, so they call off where survival is worth more than the pot. See leak LEAK-023 for the remediation path.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Practice & assess
rows.BodyText: Drill this with quiz Q00996. Review ranges at ICM-0001.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: ICM Deep Dive: Overview
rows.BodyText: Need more equity to stack off near pay jumps. Ratio of $ lost busting to $ gained winning. GTO baseline: required equity rises from ~33% (chipEV) to ~37-38% (normal bubble) up to ~61% (high bubble factor). ICM is present from hand 1 (~1.8% RP in a 200-runner).

rows.BodyText: Need more equity to stack off near pay jumps. Baseline: required equity rises from ~33% (chipEV) to ~37-38% (normal bubble) up to ~61% (high bubble factor). ICM is present from hand 1 (~1.8% RP in a 200-runner) GTO baseline: required equity rises from ~33% (chipEV) to ~37-38% (normal bubble) up to ~61% (high bubble factor). ICM is present from hand 1 (~1.8% RP in a 200-runner). Exploit: Deviate from the baseline toward the pool's specific tendency. Population: recreationals ignore ICM and call too wide → don't bluff them off, let them spew; pressure ICM-aware mid-stacks. Coach risk-premium math.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Common mistakes & fixes
rows.BodyText: Calling off chipEV-wide on bubbles; ignoring pay jumps; flatting when you should fold. Players think in chips rather than real-money equity, so they call off where survival is worth more than the pot. See leak LEAK-023 for the remediation path.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Practice & assess
rows.BodyText: Drill this with quiz Q00996. Mastery is assessed against objective LO-003. Review ranges at ICM-0001.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Final Table Dynamics: Overview
rows.BodyText: Attack covered mid-stacks relentlessly. GTO baseline: the big stack's low bubble factor lets it pressure mediums who can't risk busting — the single biggest ICM edge.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Key numbers to know
rows.BodyText: ICM chip-EV breakeven ~33.0% (Calibrated; source: GTO Wizard). Not solver-exact. ICM normal-bubble required equity ~37.5% (Calibrated; source: GTO Wizard). Not solver-exact. ICM high-bubble-factor required equity ~61.0% (Calibrated; source: GTO Wizard). Not solver-exact.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: How to apply it
rows.BodyText: Attack covered mid-stacks relentlessly. Baseline: the big stack's low bubble factor lets it pressure mediums who can't risk busting — the single biggest ICM edge GTO baseline: the big stack's low bubble factor lets it pressure mediums who can't risk busting — the single biggest ICM edge. Exploit: Deviate from the baseline toward the pool's specific tendency. Coach: pressure covered mediums, avoid coin-flips vs the cover. Exploit ICM-scared mids.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Common mistakes & fixes
rows.BodyText: Sitting passively with a big stack; tangling with the other big stack. Players think in chips rather than real-money equity, so they call off where survival is worth more than the pot. See leak LEAK-023 for the remediation path.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.BodyText: Drill this with quiz Q00996. Review ranges at ICM-0001.

rows.Heading: Heads-Up Play: Overview
rows.BodyText: Open tighter early, wider late. BB defends widest because it closes action. GTO baseline: UTG ~16-18%, HJ ~21%, CO ~25-28%, BTN ~40-44%, SB raise-or-fold. Opens are SMALLER from EP in modern solver play because more players remain to 3-bet.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Key numbers to know
rows.BodyText: UTG opens ~13.4% (RFI) at 100bb 6-max (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. HJ opens ~18.2% (RFI) at 100bb 6-max (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. CO opens ~23.3% (RFI) at 100bb 6-max (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. BTN opens ~38.5% (RFI) at 100bb 6-max (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. SB opens ~40.2% (RFI) at 100bb 6-max (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. BB defends widest because it closes action. (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: How to apply it
rows.BodyText: Open tighter early, wider late. Baseline: UTG ~16-18%, HJ ~21%, CO ~25-28%, BTN ~40-44%, SB raise-or-fold. Opens are SMALLER from EP in modern solver play because more players remain to 3-bet GTO baseline: UTG ~16-18%, HJ ~21%, CO ~25-28%, BTN ~40-44%, SB raise-or-fold. Opens are SMALLER from EP in modern solver play because more players remain to 3-bet. Exploit: tighten and let them pay you off postflop; isolate their limps Population: low-stakes opens are too loose & too big from EP. Exploit: tighten and let them pay you off postflop; isolate their limps. Coach by drilling position-by-position to 90%.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Common mistakes & fixes
rows.BodyText: Opening too wide from EP; using one big size from every seat; limping outside the SB. Players lean on static memorized charts and ignore position, stack depth, and how many players are left to act. See leak LEAK-001 for the remediation path.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Practice & assess
rows.BodyText: Drill this with quiz Q00001. Review ranges at BVB-0001.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Satellite Play: Overview
rows.BodyText: Survival > chips above the lock. GTO baseline: once locked for a seat, chips are near-worthless — fold huge equity (even AA) if shorter stacks will likely bust first.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Key numbers to know
rows.BodyText: ICM chip-EV breakeven ~33.0% (Calibrated; source: GTO Wizard). Not solver-exact. ICM normal-bubble required equity ~37.5% (Calibrated; source: GTO Wizard). Not solver-exact. ICM high-bubble-factor required equity ~61.0% (Calibrated; source: GTO Wizard). Not solver-exact.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: How to apply it
rows.BodyText: Survival > chips above the lock. Baseline: once locked for a seat, chips are near-worthless — fold huge equity (even AA) if shorter stacks will likely bust first GTO baseline: once locked for a seat, chips are near-worthless — fold huge equity (even AA) if shorter stacks will likely bust first. Exploit: Deviate from the baseline toward the pool's specific tendency. Coach the lock threshold; exploit opponents who gamble above the lock.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Common mistakes & fixes
rows.BodyText: Accumulating above the lock; gambling for a chip lead; chipEV play in a step-payout. Players think in chips rather than real-money equity, so they call off where survival is worth more than the pot. See leak LEAK-023 for the remediation path.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Practice & assess
rows.BodyText: Drill this with quiz Q00996. Review ranges at ICM-0001.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Bubble Factor Mastery: Overview
rows.BodyText: Ratio of $ lost busting to $ gained winning. GTO baseline: mid-stacks carry the highest bubble factor (most to lose); big stacks lowest (can pressure); short stacks feel it LESS than mids.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Key numbers to know
rows.BodyText: ICM chip-EV breakeven ~33.0% (Calibrated; source: GTO Wizard). Not solver-exact. ICM normal-bubble required equity ~37.5% (Calibrated; source: GTO Wizard). Not solver-exact. ICM high-bubble-factor required equity ~61.0% (Calibrated; source: GTO Wizard). Not solver-exact.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: How to apply it
rows.BodyText: Ratio of $ lost busting to $ gained winning. Baseline: mid-stacks carry the highest bubble factor (most to lose); big stacks lowest (can pressure); short stacks feel it LESS than mids GTO baseline: mid-stacks carry the highest bubble factor (most to lose); big stacks lowest (can pressure); short stacks feel it LESS than mids. Exploit: Deviate from the baseline toward the pool's specific tendency. Coach per-opponent bubble factor; exploit by pressuring covered mids and avoiding the covering stack.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Common mistakes & fixes
rows.BodyText: Treating every opponent with one bubble factor; big stack playing scared. Players think in chips rather than real-money equity, so they call off where survival is worth more than the pot. See leak LEAK-023 for the remediation path.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Practice & assess
rows.BodyText: Drill this with quiz Q00996. Review ranges at ICM-0001.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Nash Charts: Overview
rows.BodyText: Be the shover, not the caller. Call tighter than you jam. GTO baseline: 5bb pure jam-or-fold; 10bb jam UTG ~12-13% → BTN ~38% → SB ~67-75%. Jamming ranges are wider than call-off ranges.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Key numbers to know
rows.BodyText: BTN jams ~46.0% at 10bb (Nash push/fold) (Nash-Solved; source: PokerCoaching). Model-dependent (ICM/payouts). CO jams ~33.0% at 10bb (Nash push/fold) (Nash-Solved; source: PokerCoaching). Model-dependent (ICM/payouts). SB jams ~47.8% at 10bb (Nash push/fold) (Nash-Solved; source: PokerCoaching). Model-dependent (ICM/payouts). UTG jams ~20.1% at 10bb (Nash push/fold) (Nash-Solved; source: PokerCoaching). Model-dependent (ICM/payouts). BTN jams ~46.0% at 10bb (Nash push/fold) (Nash-Solved; source: PokerCoaching). Model-dependent (ICM/payouts). CO jams ~33.0% at 10bb (Nash push/fold) (Nash-Solved; source: PokerCoaching). Model-dependent (ICM/payouts).
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: How to apply it
rows.BodyText: Be the shover, not the caller. Baseline: 5bb pure jam-or-fold; 10bb jam UTG ~12-13% → BTN ~38% → SB ~67-75%. Jamming ranges are wider than call-off ranges GTO baseline: 5bb pure jam-or-fold; 10bb jam UTG ~12-13% → BTN ~38% → SB ~67-75%. Jamming ranges are wider than call-off ranges. Exploit: Deviate from the baseline toward the pool's specific tendency. Population: opponents call jams too wide and reshove too little → jam for fold equity, value-jam vs the callers. Coach charts to 90%.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Common mistakes & fixes
rows.BodyText: Open-folding spots that should jam; min-raise/folding short; over-jamming weak offsuit aces from EP. Players cling to deep-stack habits and fear busting, instead of trusting fold equity and ante math. See leak LEAK-033 for the remediation path.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Practice & assess
rows.BodyText: Drill this with quiz Q00625. Review ranges at PF-0001.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: River Play: Overview
rows.BodyText: Defend to MDF using blockers. Value-bet hands worse hands call. GTO baseline: MDF = pot/(pot+bet) — pot bet ~50%, half-pot ~67%, 2x overbet ~33%. Call hands that unblock bluffs and beat the value:bluff ratio.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Key numbers to know
rows.BodyText: Defend to MDF using blockers. (Calibrated; source: Synthesized). Not solver-exact. Value-bet hands worse hands call. (Calibrated; source: Synthesized). Not solver-exact. Polar nut-advantage on static boards. (Calibrated; source: Synthesized). Not solver-exact. Small OOP bets to set the price. (Calibrated; source: Synthesized). Not solver-exact.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: How to apply it
rows.BodyText: Defend to MDF using blockers. Baseline: MDF = pot/(pot+bet) — pot bet ~50%, half-pot ~67%, 2x overbet ~33%. Call hands that unblock bluffs and beat the value:bluff ratio GTO baseline: MDF = pot/(pot+bet) — pot bet ~50%, half-pot ~67%, 2x overbet ~33%. Call hands that unblock bluffs and beat the value:bluff ratio. Exploit: Deviate from the baseline toward the pool's specific tendency. Population: vs under-bluffing pools, fold more to big river bets; vs over-bluffers, call light. CONTESTED which pools do which — verify.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Common mistakes & fixes
rows.BodyText: Calling by absolute hand strength; over-folding vs aggression; over-calling vs under-bluffers. Players judge hands by absolute strength and auto-pilot, instead of reading board texture and whose range the board favors. See leak LEAK-012 for the remediation path.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Practice & assess
rows.BodyText: Drill this with quiz QA1014. Mastery is assessed against objective LO-009. Review ranges at RIV-0001.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Board Texture Mastery: Overview
rows.BodyText: Classify who the board favors. GTO baseline: A/K/Q-high & paired boards favor the aggressor (range bet); low/connected favor the caller (check more). Dynamic boards → bet bigger for protection.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Key numbers to know
rows.BodyText: Classify who the board favors. (Calibrated; source: Synthesized). Not solver-exact.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: How to apply it
rows.BodyText: Classify who the board favors. Baseline: A/K/Q-high & paired boards favor the aggressor (range bet); low/connected favor the caller (check more). Dynamic boards → bet bigger for protection GTO baseline: A/K/Q-high & paired boards favor the aggressor (range bet); low/connected favor the caller (check more). Dynamic boards → bet bigger for protection. Exploit: attack capped ranges on aggressor-favored turns Coach: drill 40 flops labeling favored range. Exploit: attack capped ranges on aggressor-favored turns.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Common mistakes & fixes
rows.BodyText: Treating all boards the same; barreling caller-favored boards. Players judge hands by absolute strength and auto-pilot, instead of reading board texture and whose range the board favors. See leak LEAK-012 for the remediation path.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Practice & assess
rows.BodyText: Drill this with quiz QA0406. Mastery is assessed against objective LO-020. Review ranges at FCB-0001.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.BodyText: Defend to MDF using blockers. GTO baseline: MDF = pot/(pot+bet) — pot bet ~50%, half-pot ~67%, 2x overbet ~33%. Call hands that unblock bluffs and beat the value:bluff ratio.

rows.Heading: Sizing Theory: Overview
rows.BodyText: ~2-2.5bb online, 3bb+ live. Small on range boards, polar on static. GTO baseline: ~2bb EP, ~2.3bb CO, ~2.5bb BTN, 3bb SB online; live 3-4bb due to more callers.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Key numbers to know
rows.BodyText: UTG opens ~13.4% (RFI) at 100bb 6-max (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. HJ opens ~18.2% (RFI) at 100bb 6-max (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. CO opens ~23.3% (RFI) at 100bb 6-max (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. BTN opens ~38.5% (RFI) at 100bb 6-max (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. SB opens ~40.2% (RFI) at 100bb 6-max (Calibrated; source: Derived from calibrated ranges). Not solver-exact. 3-Bet Pot In Position: flop c-bet ~71.0% (avg over boards) (Calibrated; source: Upswing / Run It Once / Janda). Not solver-exact.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: How to apply it
rows.BodyText: ~2-2.5bb online, 3bb+ live. Baseline: ~2bb EP, ~2.3bb CO, ~2.5bb BTN, 3bb SB online; live 3-4bb due to more callers GTO baseline: ~2bb EP, ~2.3bb CO, ~2.5bb BTN, 3bb SB online; live 3-4bb due to more callers. Exploit: Deviate from the baseline toward the pool's specific tendency. Population: live players call anything, so size up for value; online regs punish large opens with 3-bets. Coach to match size to fold equity & rake.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Common mistakes & fixes
rows.BodyText: Over-sizing opens online; under-sizing live where many players call. Players lean on static memorized charts and ignore position, stack depth, and how many players are left to act. See leak LEAK-012 for the remediation path.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Practice & assess
rows.BodyText: Drill this with quiz QA0406. Review ranges at FCB-0001.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Exploiting Stations: Overview
rows.BodyText: Don't fold — value relentlessly. Tendency: call far too wide, rarely fold, under-raise. Exploit: value-bet thin & big, cut bluffs to near zero.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Key numbers to know
rows.BodyText: Don't fold — value relentlessly. (Calibrated; source: Synthesized). Not solver-exact.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: How to apply it
rows.BodyText: Don't fold — value relentlessly. Baseline: Tendency: call far too wide, rarely fold, under-raise. Tendency: call far too wide, rarely fold, under-raise. Exploit: value-bet thin & big, cut bluffs to near zero. Exploit: Deviate from the baseline toward the pool's specific tendency. Coach 'who calls worse?' before betting. Highest-EV adjustment at low stakes.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Common mistakes & fixes
rows.BodyText: Bluffing stations; checking back thin value. Players assume opponents play GTO instead of adjusting to how the specific pool actually deviates. See leak LEAK-053 for the remediation path.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Practice & assess
rows.BodyText: Drill this with quiz Q01416. Mastery is assessed against objective LO-012. Review ranges at PSRPIP-0001.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Exploiting Nits: Overview
rows.BodyText: Steal & bluff relentlessly. Tendency: too tight, fold too much, only continue with strong hands. Exploit: steal wide, c-bet/barrel more, fold to their aggression.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Key numbers to know
rows.BodyText: Steal & bluff relentlessly. (Calibrated; source: Synthesized). Not solver-exact.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: How to apply it
rows.BodyText: Steal & bluff relentlessly. Baseline: Tendency: too tight, fold too much, only continue with strong hands. Tendency: too tight, fold too much, only continue with strong hands. Exploit: steal wide, c-bet/barrel more, fold to their aggression. Exploit: Deviate from the baseline toward the pool's specific tendency. Coach: attack their folds, believe their aggression.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Common mistakes & fixes
rows.BodyText: Paying off a nit's big bets; bluffing into their value. Players assume opponents play GTO instead of adjusting to how the specific pool actually deviates. See leak LEAK-043 for the remediation path.
rows.Author: Tay

rows.Heading: Practice & assess
rows.BodyText: Drill this with quiz Q01416. Review ranges at PSRPIP-0001.
rows.Author: Tay
